top_signals.title: Big Tech crash, oil volatility rattles markets: Will Bitcoin hold above $60K?
top_signals.summary: With $1.9 billion exiting the spot Bitcoin ETFs and tech stocks under pressure, BTC is failing as a hedge and at risk of falling below the $60,000 support.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Turkey’s Quiet Realignment
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
top_signals.summary: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Coinbase for Agents sets up dedicated accounts for AI bots to trade and pay for users
top_signals.summary: AI agents will get access to trade, manage money, and make payments on behalf of Coinbase users via dedicated accounts.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Most planned natural gas pipeline capacity additions in 2026 and 2027 originate in Texas
top_signals.summary: Developers plan to bring approximately 44.9 billion cubic feet per day (Bcf/d) of new pipeline capacity online in the United States in 2026 and 2027, according to our latest Natural Gas Pipeline Projects Tracker. Approximately 70% (31.6 Bcf/d) of this new capacity is already under construction. More than 66% (29.7 Bcf/d) of the capacity additions originate in Texas. Louisiana is second with 19% (8.4 Bcf/d) of total capacity additions.
